name: Length Penalties Make Chain-of-Thought Less Monitorable
summary: arXiv:2607.09786v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Length-penalized reinforcement learning can shorten chain-of-thought reasoning while hiding an influence that drives the model's answer. In our e...
description: arXiv:2607.09786v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Length-penalized reinforcement learning can shorten chain-of-thought reasoning while hiding an influence that drives the model's answer. In our experiments, training with length penalties does not stop misleading hints from steering models, even though the models' chains of thought mention the hint much less often. A token-accuracy evaluation...
author: Bryce Little
